ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Teochew cold crab. Mapped to the closest parent class 'crab food product' (FOODON_03301116) as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW (Crab, cooked) is the closest defensible match. Teochew cold crab is steamed then chilled; no specific FoodEx2 code exists for this preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for crab as a prepared dish.

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 300 g whole crab with shell)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Teochew Cold Crab is approximately 300 g of whole steamed-and-chilled crab, yielding roughly 120–150 g of edible meat and roe after shell removal.

nutrient_source.title: Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171975)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No USDA or HPB entry exists specifically for Teochew cold crab. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat' (FDC ID 171975), which is the closest approved-source equivalent for steamed crab meat. Sodium value is set to 350 mg/100 g reflecting the plain-steamed preparation without added salt. All values cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA crustacean data. Saturated fat estimated at 0.24 g/100 g based on USDA fatty acid profile for blue crab (approximately 13% of total fat). Cholesterol estimated at 78 mg/100 g per USDA blue crab profile.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed via USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171975)
gi_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
gi_source.source_match_notes: Teochew Cold Crab contains negligible carbohydrates (0.5 g/100 g per USDA SR Legacy blue crab cooked profile). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value has been assigned or tested for this food in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature, consistent with standard practice for very low-carbohydrate seafood. Glycemic load per 300 g serving approximated as 0.0 given carbohydrate content of approximately 1.5 g per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 350 mg/100 g; potassium 260 mg/100 g; fermented plum sauce condiment adds additional sodium
hypertension.guidance: Plain steamed crab provides moderate sodium (350 mg/100 g) alongside beneficial potassium (260 mg/100 g) and magnesium (35 mg/100 g); individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the accompanying fermented plum sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content (0.5 g/100 g); high protein (18.5 g/100 g); glycemic index not applicable
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teochew Cold Crab is an excellent protein source with negligible carbohydrate content and no meaningful glycemic impact, making it well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; the accompanying sweet fermented plum sauce should be used sparingly to avoid unnecessary glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (1.8 g/100 g), low saturated fat (0.24 g/100 g), omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids present, high selenium (38.5 mcg/100 g) and vitamin B12 (8.5 mcg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teochew Cold Crab is low in total and saturated fat, provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids and selenium, and is a lean high-protein food suitable for cardiovascular disease management; cholesterol content (~78 mg/100 g) is moderate and unlikely to be clinically significant in the context of an overall heart-healthy diet, though individuals on strict dietary cholesterol restrictions should monitor portion size.

gout_and_high_purine_diet.key_factor: Shellfish and crustaceans are moderate-to-high purine foods
gout_and_high_purine_diet.guidance: Individuals with gout or on a strict low-purine diet should limit or avoid Teochew Cold Crab, as crustaceans contain moderate-to-high levels of purines that can elevate serum uric acid and precipitate gout flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teochew Cold Crab (潮州冻蟹, Cháozhōu dòngxiè) is a celebrated dish of Teochew cuisine originating from Chaozhou, Guangdong Province, China. Large, meaty crabs — typically mud crabs (Scylla serrata) or flower crabs (Portunus pelagicus) — are steamed whole until just cooked through, then immediately transferred to an ice bath or refrigerator to chill. The rapid chilling firms the delicate white crab meat to a clean, springy texture and solidifies the rich, golden crab roe and hepatopancreas (creamy fat), concentrating their buttery, briny flavour. The dish is served cold, chopped into large shell-on pieces, and presented simply to showcase the natural sweetness and oceanic freshness of the crab. It is traditionally accompanied by Teochew fermented plum sauce (梅膏酱) or ginger-infused rice vinegar, whose sweet-sour and pungent notes provide a classic contrast to the crab's delicate flavour. Nutritionally, Teochew Cold Crab is a high-protein (18.5 g/100 g), low-fat (1.8 g/100 g), low-carbohydrate (0.5 g/100 g) food with approximately 95 kcal per 100 g. It is an excellent source of vitamin B12 (8.5 mcg/100 g), selenium (38.5 mcg/100 g), zinc (3.2 mg/100 g), phosphorus (185 mg/100 g), and folate (45 mcg/100 g). The glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content. Clinically, it is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease; sodium content (350 mg/100 g) warrants attention for those with hypertension, particularly when the fermented plum sauce condiment is used. Shellfish allergy and gout (high purine content) are the primary contraindications.

confidence_reason: No approved source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains an entry specifically for Teochew cold crab as a prepared dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat' (FDC ID 171975) as the closest approved-source equivalent, cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA crustacean data. The plain-steamed-and-chilled preparation is nutritionally close to the USDA reference, but minor differences in crab species (mud crab vs. blue crab) and the presence of crab roe introduce uncertainty. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE ADAPTATION: No approved source contains a specific entry for Teochew cold crab. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat' (FDC ID 171975) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA crustacean data. Saturated fat (0.24 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (0.27 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.54 g/100 g) estimated from USDA blue crab fatty acid profile. Cholesterol (78 mg/100 g) estimated from USDA blue crab profile. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: water_g is set to -1: detailed moisture data for Teochew cold crab (steamed then chilled) is not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 171975) or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific preparation as of 2024. 3. GLYCEMIC INDEX: glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category is 'not_applicable' because Teochew Cold Crab contains negligible carbohydrates (0.5 g/100 g); glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving approximated as 0.0. 4. DIETARY TAGS CORRECTION: The input record incorrectly tagged this dish as 'vegetarian' and 'vegan'. These tags have been removed as crab is an animal product. 5. PORTIONS: The input contained two identical duplicate portion entries (both '1 serving (300g)', 300 g). Only one default portion is retained. 6. HALAL STATUS: Crab is considered haram (forbidden) in some schools of Islamic jurisprudence (notably Hanafi); is_halal is set to false as a conservative default. 7. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03301116 ('crab food product') used as closest_parent match; FoodEx2 A0CJW ('Crab, cooked') used as closest_parent match. No SNOMED CT food concept found. No ontology codes are invented. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. 9. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: sugar_g is naturally zero as this is a plain steamed seafood dish with no added sugars. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as crab meat contains no dietary fiber. trans_fat_g is naturally zero as crustaceans naturally contain negligible trans fats. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy blue crab profile.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Teochew Cold Crab' is precise and unambiguous. Chinese simplified and traditional forms present. This is a Teochew-Chinese dish; Singaporean multilingual requirements (Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply as this is not a Singaporean dish by origin.
